steps.title: Use the equation already solved for $y$
steps.content: Because the first equation is already $y=9x+12$, substitute that expression into $x+7y=20$: $$x+7(9x+12)=20.$$

steps.title: Check the result
steps.content: A graphing check of the two lines shows the same intersection, $(-1,3)$. The question asks for the $y$-value, so the answer is 3.
